title: 11 Herbal Tinctures For Dry Skin
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to soothe dry skin, and they work by drawing on the natural properties of certain plants. For example, Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation in the skin.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-itching properties that can provide relief from dry skin. When you apply a licorice root tincture to dry skin, it can help calm the itch and reduce the inflammation.  Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by dryness and irritation. This can lead to healthier, more resilient skin that's better equipped to withstand dry conditions.  Using herbal tinctures for dry skin can have several benefits. They can be more gentle than over-the-counter creams and lotions, which can contain harsh chemicals that can irritate the skin further. They can also be more effective, as they address the root causes of dry skin rather than just treating the symptoms.  Another benefit of using herbal tinctures is that they can be customized to your specific skin type and needs. By combining different herbs in a tincture, you can create a remedy that addresses your specific dry skin issues.  To use an herbal tincture for dry skin, simply apply a few drops to the affected area with a cotton ball or swab. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for an all-over skin treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect dry skin. The flavonoids in calendula tincture, such as quercetin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, while the carotenoids, like beta-carotene, act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells. The anti-inflammatory properties of triterpenoid saponins help to reduce redness and irritation, promoting a healthy skin environment. By applying calendula tincture to dry skin, the bioactive constituents work together to calm, protect, and nourish the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers and 2 cups of vodka or 80% alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Calendula flowers to a separate glass jar and pour in 1 cup of glycerin.
Close both jars and store them in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking them daily.
Strain the liquid from both jars through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into separate containers, discarding the solids.
Mix the two tinctures together and store the final Calendula tincture in a dark glass bottle in the fridge for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help to soothe and hydrate dry skin. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to increase the production of natural moisturizing factors in the skin, which helps to lock in moisture and reduce dryness. The triterpenoids also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy skin cell function. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures, people with dry skin may experience improved hydration and a reduction in dryness and irritation.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 2 cups of 80% vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried root with 2 cups of vodka or glycerin in the glass jar. Make sure the root is completely covered.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the root to infuse into the liquid.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the root.
Label the jar with the date and the name of the tincture. Store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the tincture mixed with water or oil as needed for dry skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures contains bioactive constituents like histamine, acetylcholine, and mucilages, which are known for their soothing properties. The histamine in Urtica dioica tinctures helps to improve blood flow to the skin, which is beneficial for dry skin. The mucilages, a type of soluble fiber, form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing water loss. The acetylcholine in Urtica dioica tinctures also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin. By providing a protective barrier and improving blood flow, Urtica dioica tinctures can help to alleviate dry skin symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of water to the jar and mix well. Let it sit for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of glycerin to the liquid and mix well. This helps preserve the tincture and soothe dry skin.
Label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Shake the jar well before using. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or applied topically to affected areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains aloin, aloe-emodin, and other biologically active constituents. These compounds help to soothe and hydrate dry skin. Aloin and aloe-emodin have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and irritation. The tannins in aloe barbadensis help to lock in moisture, protecting the skin from water loss. The gel-like consistency of the tincture also helps to create a barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing dryness.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the Aloe gel and vodka in the jar. Stir well to mix.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Aloe tincture in the fridge. Use 10-15 drops on dry skin as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures contains compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and geranial. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Linalool, in particular, helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, while linalyl acetate and geranial have moisturizing effects that can help to lock in moisture. The antioxidant properties of these compounds also help to protect the skin from environmental stressors that can contribute to dryness. By using Melissa officinalis tinctures, you can help to calm and moisturize dry skin, promoting a healthy and balanced complexion.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Melissa officinalis flowers to the jar and pour the vodka over them, making sure they are completely covered.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 10-20 drops in a carrier oil or cream for dry skin, 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains hyperforin and hypericin, two bioactive constituents that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and calm dry skin, reducing redness and irritation. Hyperforin also has a moisturizing effect, helping to lock in moisture and protect the skin from environmental stressors. Hypericin has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can exacerbate dry skin. The combination of these properties in Hypericum perforatum tinctures makes it a useful natural remedy for dry skin.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves. Clean them with water.
Combine the cleaned flowers and leaves with 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar. Make sure the flowers are fully covered.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 10-20 drops as needed to soothe dry skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains several bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polyphenols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect dry skin. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, reducing redness and irritation. Caffeic acid derivatives have antioxidant properties that can help repair and protect the skin's natural barrier, while polyphenols can help lock in moisture and support skin health. By using Echinacea purpurea tinctures, you may find that your dry skin becomes more hydrated and resilient.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Echinacea flowers to the jar and fill the jar with vodka, making sure the flowers are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed for dry skin relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosa rubiginosa tinctures contains bioactive constituents like salicylic acid, phenolic acids, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm dry skin. The tannins in Rosa rubiginosa also help to reduce inflammation and lock in moisture, making it easier to retain hydration in the skin. The flavonoids present in the tincture have a protective effect on the skin, shielding it from environmental stressors and damage. By using Rosa rubiginosa tinctures, you can help to nourish and protect your skin, promoting a healthier and more balanced complexion.
remedies.image_alt: Rosa rubiginosa herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Rosa rubiginosa flowers and 2 cups of 80% vodka.
Combine the dried flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar. Close the jar tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Rosa rubiginosa tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops per application for dry skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures contains the bioactive constituents allantoin and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Allantoin helps to soften and soothe dry skin, reducing irritation and inflammation, while rosmarinic acid has a protective effect, shielding the skin from environmental stressors and promoting skin elasticity. The tannins present in Symphytum officinale tinctures also have a gentle astringent effect, which can help to balance the skin's moisture levels and reduce dryness. Regular use of Symphytum officinale tinctures can lead to improved skin hydration and a reduction in the appearance of dry, flaky skin.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers, or 1/2 cup of dried ones.
Combine the Symphytum officinale with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops as needed for dry skin relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe and hydrate dry skin. The aloe-emodin in aloe vera tinctures has been shown to reduce redness and inflammation, while the vitamins A and C help repair and protect the skin's natural barrier. The aloin in aloe vera tinctures also has a cooling effect, which can help calm irritated skin and reduce dryness. By reducing inflammation and promoting hydration, aloe vera tinctures can help alleviate dry skin symptoms and promote healthier-looking skin.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh aloe vera gel and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the aloe vera gel to the jar and top it with vodka, leaving 1 inch of space at the top.
Close the jar and shake it well for 10 seconds to mix the ingredients.
Place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures contains bioactive constituents like cineole, limonene, and terpinen-4-ol, which give it anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to improve skin hydration and reduce water loss. Limonene has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from environmental stressors, while terpinen-4-ol can help to soothe and calm dry, itchy skin. By reducing inflammation and protecting the skin from damage, Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures can help to relieve dry skin symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal tinctures for dry skin
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of 80-proof vodka.
Combine the leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are fully covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place, using 10-20 drops as needed for dry skin relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea can help prevent dry skin. Some teas, like chamomile and peppermint, have soothing properties that can calm and hydrate the skin. They can also help lock in moisture and reduce inflammation. Regular consumption may lead to softer, healthier-looking skin.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for dry skin can be a good choice, but drinking them every day might not be the best idea. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally safe in moderation, but too much can cause problems. It's essential to listen to your body and adjust your tea consumption accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with dry skin, but it takes some time to see results. Drinking herbal teas like calendula, chamomile, and peppermint can help soothe dry skin. It may take a few days to a week to notice a difference, as the antioxidants and moisturizing properties in the teas start to work.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for dry skin is best in the morning. This helps to moisturize your skin throughout the day. You can also drink it before bedtime to help repair and hydrate your skin while you sleep. Try to avoid drinking it too close to mealtime, as this can cause digestive issues.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for dry skin. Some teas, like ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort, may affect blood thinners and skin creams. Others, like licorice root and chamomile, can alter how your body absorbs certain medications. This can change how your skin responds to treatment.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have dry skin, some herbal teas might help. But be careful - some can be too strong. For example, peppermint and ginger teas can stimulate the uterus, which isn't good during pregnancy. It's best to choose gentle teas like chamomile or calendula, but always check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for dry skin can be a good option while breastfeeding, but be cautious. Some herbs, like calendula and chamomile, are safe and may help soothe dry skin. However, others like comfrey and pennyroyal can pass into breast milk and might cause issues. Always check the ingredients and look for teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding.
title_2: Nursing Home Residents' Guide to 11 Herbal Tinctures for Dry Skin
